title: Detected stack: IIS + ASP.NET (Web Forms)
evidence: <configuration> with <system.web>/<system.webServer>, Forms auth, ASP.NET 4.8; companion applicationHost.config.fragment and Default.aspx
notes: Stack summary only; drives IIS reference pack + auth/secrets checks.

title: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 enabled on IIS binding
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Disable TLS 1.0/1.1, enable TLS 1.2+  — addresses CWE-326

Before (dangerous):

<system.webServer>
  <security>
    <access sslFlags="Ssl" />
  </security>
</system.webServer>
<!-- with SCHANNEL registry leaving Tls10 / Tls11 enabled -->

After (safe):

<system.webServer>
  <security>
    <access sslFlags="Ssl, Ssl128" />
  </security>
</system.webServer>
<!-- Plus SCHANNEL registry: disable TLS 1.0 / TLS 1.1 server-side, enable
     TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 (Server 2022). Per RFC 9325, TLS 1.2 is the minimum. -->
notes: Tls10 and Tls11 explicitly listed alongside Tls12; RFC 8996/9325 deprecate both.

title: Directory browsing enabled
notes: No dedicated Fix recipes block in iis.md for directoryBrowse; set fix_recipe=null per spec. Remediation: set directoryBrowse enabled="false" and rely on <defaultDocument>.

title: Server and X-Powered-By headers not suppressed
evidence: <httpProtocol><customHeaders>...<add name="Cache-Control" value="no-cache" /></customHeaders></httpProtocol> — no <remove name="Server"> and no <remove name="X-Powered-By">
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Remove Server and X-Powered-By headers  — addresses CWE-200

Before (dangerous):

<system.webServer>
  <!-- no customHeaders block; IIS emits Server: Microsoft-IIS/10.0
       and ASP.NET emits X-Powered-By: ASP.NET -->
</system.webServer>

After (safe):

<system.webServer>
  <httpProtocol>
    <customHeaders>
      <remove name="Server" />
      <remove name="X-Powered-By" />
    </customHeaders>
  </httpProtocol>
  <security>
    <requestFiltering removeServerHeader="true" />
  </security>
</system.webServer>
notes: customHeaders block present but omits remove entries for Server / X-Powered-By.

title: ASP.NET customErrors mode="Off" leaks stack traces
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Harden request filtering and custom errors  — addresses CWE-20 / CWE-209

Before (dangerous):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="Off" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="Detailed" />
  <security>
    <requestFiltering>
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="2147483647" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>

After (safe):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="RemoteOnly" defaultRedirect="~/Error" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="DetailedLocalOnly" existingResponse="Replace">
    <remove statusCode="500" />
    <error statusCode="500" path="/Error/500" responseMode="ExecuteURL" />
  </httpErrors>
  <security>
    <requestFiltering allowDoubleEscaping="false" allowHighBitCharacters="false">
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="30000000"
                     maxUrl="4096"
                     maxQueryString="2048" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>
notes: appSettings marks Environment=Production; not a debug-only transform.

title: IIS httpErrors errorMode="Detailed" returns full errors to remote clients
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Harden request filtering and custom errors  — addresses CWE-20 / CWE-209

Before (dangerous):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="Off" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="Detailed" />
  <security>
    <requestFiltering>
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="2147483647" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>

After (safe):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="RemoteOnly" defaultRedirect="~/Error" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="DetailedLocalOnly" existingResponse="Replace">
    <remove statusCode="500" />
    <error statusCode="500" path="/Error/500" responseMode="ExecuteURL" />
  </httpErrors>
  <security>
    <requestFiltering allowDoubleEscaping="false" allowHighBitCharacters="false">
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="30000000"
                     maxUrl="4096"
                     maxQueryString="2048" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>
notes: errorMode=Detailed returns full IIS error pages to remote callers; should be DetailedLocalOnly.

title: Anonymous authentication runs as shared built-in IUSR
notes: No dedicated Fix recipes block for anonymous-auth app-pool isolation in iis.md; remediation: use a per-app-pool identity (ApplicationPoolIdentity) rather than the shared IUSR account.

title: machineKey validationKey/decryptionKey set to AutoGenerate
notes: No dedicated Fix recipes block for machineKey in iis.md; remediation: generate explicit validationKey/decryptionKey hex values identical across farm nodes.

title: requestFiltering maxAllowedContentLength ~1 GB enables large-body DoS
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Harden request filtering and custom errors  — addresses CWE-20 / CWE-209

Before (dangerous):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="Off" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="Detailed" />
  <security>
    <requestFiltering>
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="2147483647" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>

After (safe):

<system.web>
  <customErrors mode="RemoteOnly" defaultRedirect="~/Error" />
</system.web>
<system.webServer>
  <httpErrors errorMode="DetailedLocalOnly" existingResponse="Replace">
    <remove statusCode="500" />
    <error statusCode="500" path="/Error/500" responseMode="ExecuteURL" />
  </httpErrors>
  <security>
    <requestFiltering allowDoubleEscaping="false" allowHighBitCharacters="false">
      <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="30000000"
                     maxUrl="4096"
                     maxQueryString="2048" />
    </requestFiltering>
  </security>
</system.webServer>
notes: 999999999 bytes (~1 GB) far above the 30 MB default; allowDoubleEscaping="true" also weakens filtering.

title: Missing security response headers (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options)
evidence: <customHeaders>...<add name="Cache-Control" value="no-cache" /></customHeaders> — no Strict-Transport-Security, no X-Content-Type-Options, no X-Frame-Options
fix_recipe:
Recipe: Enforce HSTS and security response headers  — addresses CWE-319 / CWE-1021

Before (dangerous):

<system.webServer>
  <httpProtocol>
    <customHeaders />
  </httpProtocol>
</system.webServer>

After (safe):

<system.webServer>
  <httpProtocol>
    <customHeaders>
      <add name="Strict-Transport-Security" value="max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload" />
      <add name="X-Content-Type-Options" value="nosniff" />
      <add name="X-Frame-Options" value="DENY" />
      <add name="Referrer-Policy" value="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" />
      <add name="Content-Security-Policy" value="default-src 'self'" />
    </customHeaders>
  </httpProtocol>
</system.webServer>
notes: customHeaders block defines only Cache-Control; baseline security headers absent.
